The output of one AI/agent call is passed directly as input to another AI/agent call with no validation Pre-Node in between -- the receiving call is 'seeded' with unchecked context. Directly computed from Pass 4 (AgentHandoverAnalyser) results where pre_node_exists is false.

AI output is written back into its own memory/history/context store and reused as input to a subsequent call -- a self-referential loop where corrupted output can re-seed and compound itself across turns.

Two or more AI calls are chained within the same file/module with no traceable validation between them -- if the first call's output is corrupted, the cascade has no origin check to catch it. Directly computed from the existing 'chained AI calls' DC-E13 finding (count(ai_integrations) > 1).

Code directly maximises a metric/score/reward variable rather than the underlying objective the metric was meant to proxy. Co-occurrence with a consequential action and no independent correctness check raises confidence.
Behaviour branches on whether the system is being observed/monitored/tested rather than on the task itself -- a structural precondition for divergence between monitored and unmonitored behaviour.

A confidence/probability/score threshold check gates a consequential action (e.g. 'if output.confidence > threshold: approve(output)') with no independent correctness check alongside it. The decision optimises for the metric (confidence) rather than for correctness -- matches QA contract test case 3 (DC-I11 / L3: Metric Saturation).

A validation/check call is present and its result is computed, but the consequential action that follows does not branch on that result -- the system appears governed (the check runs) while the gate has no enforcement effect (Shadow Compliance).

A single agent-to-agent handover (Pass 4) where pre_node_exists is false -- the receiving agent gets the previous agent's output with no validation gate. Directly computed, not inferred.

A sequential-agent pipeline (Pass 5) with two or more ungated handovers -- a corrupted output at any stage propagates through the rest of the pipeline. Directly computed from ClusterGovernanceAnalyser results.
